evidence_quotes.quote: When I buy a game, it shouldnt be switched off later.
Always-online and server kills take away what I paid for.
If servers must close, keep the game playable offline or enable community servers.
If a game depends on servers, show it clearly and state how long support will last.
Set a minimum support period (e.g., years from last EU sale) or a mandatory offline fallback at end-of-life.
Consumers get repair (offline mode), replacement, or a refundautomatically.
That feels like an unfair contract term and a clear lack of conformity: the product no longer does the basic thing it was sold to dobe playable.

paragraphs.text: When I buy a game, it shouldnt be switched off later. Always-online and server kills take away what I paid for. That feels like an unfair contract term and a clear lack of conformity: the product no longer does the basic thing it was sold to dobe playable. Please close this loophole. Require publishers to: Never brick a purchased game. If servers must close, keep the game playable offline or enable community servers. Treat shutdowns as non-conformity. Consumers get repair (offline mode), replacement, or a refundautomatically. Be honest at purchase. If a game depends on servers, show it clearly and state how long support will last. Set a minimum support period (e.g., years from last EU sale) or a mandatory offline fallback at end-of-life. Games are cultural works and peoples purchases should be respected. Killing a game after sale undermines trust in the digital single market.
